heading: Citizens as pirates
label: 1652 Citizens as pirates
text: Whoever, being a citizen of the United States, commits any murder or robbery, or any act of hostility against the United States, or against any citizen thereof, on the high seas, under color of any commission from any foreign prince, or state, or on pretense of authority from any person, is a pirate, and shall be imprisoned for life.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 495 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 30435 Stat. 1147Words “Notwithstanding the pretense of such authority,” were omitted as surplusage.
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 495 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 30435 Stat. 1147Words “Notwithstanding the pretense of such authority,” were omitted as surplusage.
